Rosa rubiginosa, also known as Sweet Briar or Eglantine, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include treating digestive issues, colds, and flu due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Rosa rubiginosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Rosa rubiginosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
